steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$p \left(x\right)+57={x}^{2}$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $-57$.

steps.title: Read the vertex directly
steps.content: Written as $p(x)=(x-0)^2+(-57)$, this is vertex form $(h,k)=(0,-57)$. Since $a=1\gt 0$, the parabola opens upward — the vertex is the minimum.

steps.title: State the minimum value
steps.content: The minimum value is $p(0)=-57$. This is the output value at the vertex, not the $x$-coordinate of the vertex. B is correct.
